summary: Tests added for both branches of the new NotRecordable filter (positive: filtered out and counted; negative: passes through). Existing tests updated for the new total view count and the filter delta. Test_app_record was refactored from a hardcoded len(view.New())+1 calculation to use a countRecordable() helper so it stays correct as more views are added in later tasks. No redundant tests, no excessive mocking, behavior-focused assertions.

checks.pyramidPlacement: pass — unit tests on filterViews (pure function over a map), no integration/E2E indirection
checks.behaviorNotImplementation: pass — asserts on output map size, key membership, registered view metadata (Ncols=17, NotRecordable=true)
checks.edgeCases: pass — TestFilterViews_NotRecordable runs with version=0 and empty pgssSchema to prove NotRecordable short-circuits before the other checks; TestFilterViews_Recordable proves the zero-value default keeps a view
checks.redundantTesting: none — each test exercises a distinct contract: filterViews short-circuit, filterViews pass-through, view.New() registration sanity
checks.tddSequence: followed — tests written first, observed compile failure (unknown field NotRecordable), then implementation added until tests pass
checks.testNaming: consistent with file conventions (Test_filterViews uses snake_case from before; the two new tests use TestXxx_Y camelCase — both styles already present in the file)
checks.fixturesAndMocks: no mocks introduced; tests use small in-memory view.Views maps
checks.regressionRisk: Test_app_record now derives its expected file count from view.New() via countRecordable() rather than a hardcoded constant — adding future views (recordable or not) will not silently break this test
checks.assertionsRealBehavior: pass — assert.Equal on filtered count, assert.NotContains on map keys, assert.True on registered field; no mock-call-only assertions
checks.isolation: pass — TestFilterViews_NotRecordable and TestFilterViews_Recordable each construct their own minimal view.Views fixture, no shared state
